item.title: Govt monitoring ‘Strait of Hormuz': Muktadir
item.description: Commerce Minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir has stated that the government is closely monitoring the situation regarding the Strait of Hormuz, emphasizing that while it is a critical global trade route, there is no immediate cause for concern regarding the import of essential commodities and fuel.

item.title: Govt proposes opposition nominee for Deputy Speaker: Salahuddin
item.description: Home Minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Tuesday said the government has proposed that the post of Deputy Speaker of Parliament be offered to a nominee from the opposition party, in line with the spirit of the July Charter. “Respecting the July Charter, the government has proposed that the oppositi

item.title: BB buys $25m thru auction
item.description: Bangladesh Bank (BB) on Monday purchased US$25 million from two commercial banks through multiple auction methods. According to central bank data, it bought dollars at the rate of Tk 122.30. Accordingly, total purchases stood at $5,493.50 million in FY 2025-26 till March 02. Sources said the BB

item.title: Security tightened in Dhaka’s diplomatic area: Home Minister
item.description: Security has been strengthened in Dhaka’s diplomatic area, including around the United States Embassy, amid the current global situation, Home Minister Salahuddin Ahmed said on Monday. He made the remarks during a courtesy call by US Ambassador to Bangladesh, Brent T. Christensen, at his min

item.title: Bangladesh, India agree to work towards boosting bilateral trade
item.description: Bangladesh and India have agreed to work towards expanding bilateral trade, Commerce Minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir said on Monday. “Trade between the two countries has faced various obstacles due to some mutual decisions. Both sides have drawn each other’s attention to reviewing de

item.title: Narcotics Control inspector injured in gun attack in Dhaka
item.description: An inspector of Department of Narcotics Control (DNC) was shot and injured in an attack allegedly by drug peddlers at Saidabad Bus Terminal in the capital on Monday. The victim was identified as Siddiqur Rahman, 38. Rajiur Rahman, Deputy Director of DNC (South Circle), said as part of an anti-nar

item.title: No sign Iran's nuclear sites were hit, IAEA says, but Iran alleges one was
item.description: The UN nuclear watchdog has no indication Israeli and US attacks on Iran have hit any nuclear facilities, its chief Rafael Grossi told the agency's Board of Governors on Monday, moments before Iran's envoy said one was targeted a day earlier. Iran's nuclear programme has been among the reasons Isr

item.title: Exports fall 12.03pc in February
item.description: Bangladesh’s export earnings declined further in February this year, falling 12.03 per cent year-on-year to US$4.41 billion, according to the latest data released by the Export Promotion Bureau (EPB) on Monday. Exports stood at US$3.97 billion at the corresponding month of the previous year.

item.title: Minister assures support for crew stranded in UAE port
item.description: The government is closely monitoring the situation of the crew of a Bangladesh Shipping Corporation (BSC) vessel stranded in the United Arab Emirates amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, Shipping Minister Sheikh Rabiul Alam has said. In a video message, the minister expressed sympathy for

item.title: Sirens sound at UK's Akrotiri airbase on Cyprus, Cypriot state TV says
item.description: Sirens went off at Britain's RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus shortly after midday (on Monday, hours after it was hit by an unmanned drone, Cyprus's state TV CyBC reported. In a live broadcast CyBC reported sirens going off at the base, and aircraft taking off from the facility close to the southern ci

item.title: IDRA approves appointment of Nakibur Rahman Khan as CEO of Central Insurance
item.description: Insurance Development and Regulatory Authority (IDRA) has approved the appointment of Md Nakibur Rahman Khan as the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Central Insurance PLC. The insurance regulator confirmed his appointment in an official notification, according to a media release.

item.title: Pakistan imposes curfew after deadly protests over US-Israeli strikes on Iran
item.description: Pakistani authorities deployed troops and imposed a three-day curfew before dawn Monday in the northern cities of Gilgit and Skardu after several people died and tens were injured in violent protests following the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes,
